title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within 2% of actual ARR
anchor: Forecast ARR at $1.8M versus actual $1.77M, a 1.7% variance on the committed number.
connect: Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%, making this the tightest call in recent quarters.
body: Forecast ARR at $1.8M versus actual $1.77M, a 1.7% variance on the committed number. Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%, making this the tightest call in recent quarters.
trace: Q2 forecast accuracy improved significantly versus historical pattern, suggesting better renewal visibility or risk detection.

title: Mid-market NRR sits at 112% in Q2, up from 105% average
anchor: Mid-market customers generated 112% NRR this quarter versus the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%.
connect: Multi-product customers drove 124% NRR across 46 accounts in the same period.
body: Mid-market customers generated 112% NRR this quarter versus the trailing three-quarter average of 104.9%. Multi-product customers drove 124% NRR across 46 accounts in the same period.
trace: Mid-market NRR acceleration coincides with multi-product adoption momentum in that segment.

title: Multi-product customers compound at 124% NRR versus 102% single-product
anchor: Multi-product accounts generated 124% NRR across 46 customers in Q2, while single-product accounts held at 102% across 74 customers.
connect: Multi-product share represents 38.3% of the customer base currently.
body: Multi-product accounts generated 124% NRR across 46 customers in Q2, while single-product accounts held at 102% across 74 customers. Multi-product share represents 38.3% of the customer base currently.
trace: Clear NRR bifurcation between multi-product and single-product cohorts, with multi-product representing minority share but outsized expansion.

title: At-risk ARR pool contracts to $220K from $310K in March
anchor: April at-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts, down from $310K across 7 accounts in March.
connect: Top-20 ARR accounts in the at-risk pool decreased from 5 to 2 over the same window.
body: April at-risk pool sits at $220K across 3 accounts, down from $310K across 7 accounts in March. Top-20 ARR accounts in the at-risk pool decreased from 5 to 2 over the same window.
trace: At-risk portfolio contracted both in dollar terms and account concentration, with high-value accounts moving out of risk status.

title: Mid-market time to first value accelerates to 23 days in Q2
anchor: Q2 mid-market new customers reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus 38 days across 22 accounts in Q1.
connect: Q1 cohort renewal correlation shows under-30-day TTFV customers renewed at 91% versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
body: Q2 mid-market new customers reached first value at 23 days across 24 accounts versus 38 days across 22 accounts in Q1. Q1 cohort renewal correlation shows under-30-day TTFV customers renewed at 91% versus 74% for over-60-day TTFV.
trace: TTFV improvement in Q2 mid-market onboarding, with historical data showing strong correlation between fast TTFV and renewal rates.

title: Mid-market health scores improve to 78% green from 71% in Q1
anchor: Q2 mid-market health distribution shows 78% green accounts (45 of 58) versus 71% green (41 of 58) in Q1.
connect: Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% while yellow-health customers renewed at 75%.
body: Q2 mid-market health distribution shows 78% green accounts (45 of 58) versus 71% green (41 of 58) in Q1. Q1 green-health customers renewed at 95% while yellow-health customers renewed at 75%.
trace: Health score distribution improved in mid-market, with historical data showing strong predictive value for renewal outcomes.

title: License utilization above 80% increases to 22 mid-market accounts
anchor: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1.
connect: Atlas Insights adoption spans 24 mid-market customers, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2.
body: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization reached 22 in Q2 versus 14 in Q1. Atlas Insights adoption spans 24 mid-market customers, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2.
trace: License utilization growth in mid-market segment coincides with Atlas Insights adoption and expansion activity.

title: Coverage tier retention shows 14-point variance between high-touch and tech-touch
anchor: High-touch coverage generated 96% GRR across 22 accounts in Q2 while tech-touch coverage generated 82% GRR across 98 accounts.
connect: High-touch accounts represent 18.3% of the customer base but include top ARR concentration.
body: High-touch coverage generated 96% GRR across 22 accounts in Q2 while tech-touch coverage generated 82% GRR across 98 accounts. High-touch accounts represent 18.3% of the customer base but include top ARR concentration.
trace: Significant GRR divergence between coverage tiers, with high-touch representing minority of accounts but majority of ARR protection.

title: Customer health reviews source 8 expansion opportunities worth $340K in April
anchor: April expansion opportunities from customer health reviews totaled 8 deals worth $340K combined ARR.
connect: March customer health reviews generated 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance versus 41% acceptance for outbound expansion.
body: April expansion opportunities from customer health reviews totaled 8 deals worth $340K combined ARR. March customer health reviews generated 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance versus 41% acceptance for outbound expansion.
trace: Health review sourced expansion shows higher volume in April and historically higher sales acceptance rates than outbound expansion motions.

title: Enterprise renewal signing accelerates with 4 sponsors deepening engagement
anchor: April enterprise renewals include 9 signed accounts, with 4 showing executive sponsor depth increases during Q1.
connect: Beacon Logistics renewed $280K ARR on April 21st, three months ahead of the July 15th contract end date.
body: April enterprise renewals include 9 signed accounts, with 4 showing executive sponsor depth increases during Q1. Beacon Logistics renewed $280K ARR on April 21st, three months ahead of the July 15th contract end date.
trace: Enterprise renewal momentum with sponsor engagement correlation and early signing behavior, exemplified by Beacon's early renewal.

title: All-segments 90-day retention improves to 88% with faster onboarding
anchor: Q1 new-customer cohort across all segments achieved 88% retention at 90 days with 26-day average TTFV across 60 accounts.
connect: Q4 cohort achieved 83% retention at 90 days with 38-day average TTFV across 55 accounts.
body: Q1 new-customer cohort across all segments achieved 88% retention at 90 days with 26-day average TTFV across 60 accounts. Q4 cohort achieved 83% retention at 90 days with 38-day average TTFV across 55 accounts.
trace: Cohort retention improvement correlates with faster time to first value achievement across the full customer base.

title: Small-business NRR holds at 102% despite 86% gross retention
anchor: Q2 small-business segment generated 102% NRR and 86% GRR across $252K renewing book ARR.
connect: Mid-market segment achieved 112% NRR and 91% GRR across $1.116M renewing book in the same period.
body: Q2 small-business segment generated 102% NRR and 86% GRR across $252K renewing book ARR. Mid-market segment achieved 112% NRR and 91% GRR across $1.116M renewing book in the same period.
trace: Small-business segment shows expansion activity offsetting higher churn rates, contrasted with mid-market's stronger retention foundation.

title: Enterprise NRR compresses to 106% from multi-product momentum
anchor: Q2 enterprise customers generated 106% NRR and 94% GRR across $432K renewing book ARR.
connect: Multi-product customers achieved 124% NRR in Q2 compared to 116% in Q1 across all segments.
body: Q2 enterprise customers generated 106% NRR and 94% GRR across $432K renewing book ARR. Multi-product customers achieved 124% NRR in Q2 compared to 116% in Q1 across all segments.
trace: Enterprise NRR performance in context of overall multi-product acceleration, suggesting segment-specific expansion patterns.

title: High-touch coverage GRR improves to 96% from 94% in Q1
anchor: High-touch tier achieved 96% GRR in Q2 across 22 accounts versus 94% GRR in Q1.
connect: Tech-touch tier GRR moved from 86% in Q1 to 82% in Q2 across 98 accounts.
body: High-touch tier achieved 96% GRR in Q2 across 22 accounts versus 94% GRR in Q1. Tech-touch tier GRR moved from 86% in Q1 to 82% in Q2 across 98 accounts.
trace: Coverage tier retention trends diverging, with high-touch improving while tech-touch retention declines quarter over quarter.

title: Atlas Insights drives 75% expansion rate among mid-market adopters
anchor: Atlas Insights adoption reaches 24 mid-market customers, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2.
connect: Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization increased from 14 to 22 accounts over the same period.
body: Atlas Insights adoption reaches 24 mid-market customers, with 18 expanding ARR during Q2. Mid-market customers above 80% license utilization increased from 14 to 22 accounts over the same period.
trace: Atlas Insights shows high expansion conversion rate in mid-market, coinciding with increased license utilization across the segment.

title: Executive sponsor coverage reaches 2 of 2 top-ARR at-risk accounts
anchor: Top-20 ARR accounts in April at-risk pool number 2, both scheduled for executive sponsor review this week.
connect: At-risk pool concentration in top-ARR accounts decreased from 5 to 2 between March and April.
body: Top-20 ARR accounts in April at-risk pool number 2, both scheduled for executive sponsor review this week. At-risk pool concentration in top-ARR accounts decreased from 5 to 2 between March and April.
trace: Executive sponsor engagement covers all remaining high-value at-risk accounts as the at-risk concentration decreases.
